value: Eligibility (general): BMI ≥30 or BMI ≥27 with comorbidity (HTN, DM, OSA). Confirm lifestyle attempts + follow-up plan.
Contraindication: personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) or MEN2 → avoid GLP-1 RA.
Contraindication: pregnancy, trying to conceive, or breastfeeding → avoid GLP-1 RA.
Contraindication/caution: prior pancreatitis → generally avoid; consider alternatives and specialist input.
Caution: gallstones/biliary colic history; GLP-1s can increase gallbladder events. Counsel and monitor RUQ pain.
Caution: severe baseline GI disease or severe ongoing nausea/vomiting → hold/avoid until stable.
Stop/ED triggers: persistent vomiting, dehydration, severe abdominal pain (pancreatitis concern), jaundice/RUQ pain + fever (cholangitis), severe weakness/AMS.
Dosing note (reference): start low, titrate slowly per product label to reduce GI effects. Do not accelerate titration.
Counseling: prioritize protein, hydration, fiber; expect appetite reduction; constipation common; smaller meals; avoid high-fat large meals early.
Monitoring: weight trend, GI tolerance, hydration, BP, glucose (esp if on insulin/sulfonylurea), gallbladder symptoms. Consider periodic labs if indicated.
Diabetes note: hypoglycemia risk increases primarily when combined with insulin or sulfonylureas. Consider dose adjustment and glucose monitoring plan.
Eating disorder caution: avoid initiating for active eating disorder/unsafe restriction; ensure screening and appropriate mental health support.
Post-bariatric caution: altered tolerance/intake; ensure protein targets and deficiency surveillance; coordinate with bariatric team if initiating.
Follow-up: schedule check-in 2–4 weeks after start/titration; reinforce nutrition plan; review side effects and adherence.
